content.problemStatement:
There are NNN players standing in a line, indexed 111 to NNN from left to right. They all play a game of Rock, Paper, Scissors. Each player has already decided which move they want to play. You are given this information as a string SSS of length NNN, i.e,


SiS_iSi​ is equal to R\verb+R+R if player iii will play Rock.
SiS_iSi​ is equal to P\verb+P+P if player iii will play Paper.
SiS_iSi​ is equal to S\verb+S+S if player iii will play Scissors.
Let W(i,j)W(i, j)W(i,j) denote the move played by the winner if players i,i+1,…,ji, i+1, \ldots, ji,i+1,…,j compete in order from left to right. That is,


First, players iii and i+1i+1i+1 play a game
The winner of this game plays against player i+2i+2i+2
The winner of the second game plays against player i+3i+3i+3
⋮\vdots⋮


The winner of the first j−i−1j-i-1j−i−1 games plays against player jjj, and the move played by the winner of this game is declared to be W(i,j)W(i, j)W(i,j).
If i=ji = ji=j, then player iii is considered to be the winner and W(i,i)=SiW(i, i) = S_iW(i,i)=Si​.

Your task is to find the value of W(i,N)W(i,N)W(i,N) for all iii from 111 to NNN.

**Note** :
If a person with index iii and index jjj (i<ji \lt ji<j) play against each other, then:


If Si≠SjS_i \neq S_jSi​=Sj​, the winner is decided by classical rules, i.e, rock beats scissors, scissors beats paper, and paper beats rock.
If Si=SjS_i = S_jSi​=Sj​, the player with lower index (in this case, iii) wins.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of players.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS of length NNN, denoting the moves chosen by the players.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing a string of length NNN, whose iii-th character is W(i,N)W(i, N)W(i,N).
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of players. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting only of characters 'R', 'P' and 'S', where S_i denotes the move of the i‑th player.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing a string of length N. The i‑th character of this string is the move of the winner when the players i,i+1,…,N play the described sequential Rock‑Paper‑Scissors tournament.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case the only player plays 'S', so the winner is 'S'.\nFor the second test case the suffix winners are: i=4 -> 'R', i=3 -> 'P' (paper beats rock), i=2 -> 'S' (scissors beats paper), i=1 -> 'S' (tie, lower index wins). Hence the output string is "SSPR".

solution.approach: Process each test case from right to left. Keep the current winner of the suffix (its index and move). For position i compare S[i] with the current winner's move: • If S[i] beats the winner's move, player i becomes the new winner. • If the winner's move beats S[i], keep the current winner. • If they are equal, the lower index (i) wins, so player i becomes the winner. Store the winner's move for each i. This yields the answer for all suffixes in O(N) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the output string (can be written directly), O(1) extra.
